body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, a formal violation notice was issued today to the property owner at {ADDRESS} for accumulation of inoperable vehicles and exterior debris. Case number {CASE}, dated {DATE}. The owner has {DAYS} days to achieve compliance before we escalate to adjudication. I've attached photos taken during this morning's inspection. Let me know if you'd like a briefing before the {DATE} council packet goes out.
reply_tmpl: Sandra, thanks for the heads-up. Please add {CASE} to next week's problem-property summary so Tina can flag it for the council report. Keep me posted on compliance status.

body_tmpl: Karen, please find attached the monthly code enforcement summary for the period ending {DATE}. We opened {NUM} new cases this month, closed {NUM} cases in compliance, and referred {NUM} to adjudication. Top violation categories remain tall grass/weeds, exterior storage, and fence condition. We issued {NUM} notices on St. Charles Rd corridor properties. I'll have draft talking points ready for the mayor's briefing by end of week.

Mayor Okonkwo, providing an update on the problem property at {ADDRESS}. Since our last briefing, code has cited the owner three additional times — case numbers {CASE} and two related files. The owner appeared before the adjudication hearing officer on {DATE} and was fined {AMOUNT}. As of today the fine remains unpaid and the conditions are substantially unchanged. I'm recommending we coordinate with the building official on a potential dangerous-structure referral. Rosa and I can meet jointly with you if helpful.
reply_tmpl: Sandra and Rosa — let's get on the calendar. Tina, can you set up a 30-minute slot before the {DATE} staff meeting? I want legal counsel looped in as well.

body_tmpl: Rosa, the compliance window for {ADDRESS} closes on {DATE} — that's {DAYS} days from today. Case {CASE} covers both the unpermitted deck addition and the deteriorating facade. I'll be doing a re-inspection the morning of {DATE}. If the work isn't completed or a valid permit isn't pulled by then, I'll refer it straight to adjudication. Can you confirm whether any permit application is pending on your end?
reply_tmpl: Sandra, no permit application on file as of this morning. I ran the address — last permit was pulled in {DATE} for HVAC. If they call in now it's too late for them to pull and complete in time. I'll note that in my system and support the referral.

body_tmpl: Yolanda, attached is the proposed docket for the code adjudication hearing on {DATE} at {TIME}. We have {NUM} cases scheduled, including three first appearances and two compliance-review continuances. Please publish the notice per your standard timeline. I'll have all case files to you by {DATE}. Let me know if the hearing officer has any conflicts — we may need to swap the {TIME} slot.

Mayor, here's a recap of yesterday's adjudication hearing. The officer heard {NUM} cases. Fines totaling {AMOUNT} were assessed; {NUM} cases were granted compliance extensions of {DAYS} days; and {NUM} cases were dismissed after property owners demonstrated full compliance prior to hearing. The unresolved matter at {ADDRESS} was continued to {DATE}. Finance has been notified of the assessed amounts.
reply_tmpl: Thanks, Sandra. Can you flag the {ADDRESS} continuance separately — I want to be able to answer questions about it if residents ask. Good work on the others.

subject_tmpl: Referral from PD – Suspected Illegal Dumping at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Sandra, patrol responded to a report of large-scale debris dumping in the rear yard at {ADDRESS} on {DATE} around {TIME}. We documented tires, appliances, and construction waste. Officers spoke with the property owner and issued a trespass advisory but this is a code matter for follow-up. I'm attaching the incident report. Do you want me to have patrol swing by again on {DATE} for a joint look?
reply_tmpl: Reggie, yes — let's do a joint walkthrough at {TIME} on {DATE}. I'll open a case this morning and bring my camera gear. Please have the incident number ready so I can cross-reference.

body_tmpl: Sandra, I received a rodent harborage complaint for {ADDRESS} that I inspected yesterday. The source appears to be accumulated garbage and overgrown vegetation in the rear yard — clearly a code violation as well as a health concern. I've opened a health file under case {CASE}. Can we coordinate a joint inspection so you can open a parallel code case? I want to make sure the owner gets notices from both departments simultaneously. I'm free {DATE} morning.

Sandra, crew flagged a visibility issue on {STREET} near {CROSS} — hedges on the private property at {ADDRESS} are encroaching into the right-of-way and obscuring sight lines at the intersection. This is both a safety and a code issue. I've marked the location on the attached map. Can you issue a notice to the owner? If they don't trim by {DATE} let me know and we'll discuss having the Village do the work and bill it back.
reply_tmpl: Frank, I'll swing by today and document it. If it's what you're describing I can get a notice out by end of week. I'll copy you when it goes out.

subject_tmpl: Unpaid Adjudication Fines – Lien Referrals Needed
body_tmpl: Sandra, our accounts receivable aging shows {NUM} code adjudication fines totaling {AMOUNT} that are more than 90 days past due. Per Village policy we need signed lien authorization packages from your office before we can certify these to the Cook County tax rolls. Can you provide the relevant case files and compliance documentation for the following properties by {DATE}? I'll have my team prepare the lien resolutions for the next board meeting.

Mayor, just a brief note — we completed our seasonal tall grass sweep of the {STREET} corridor between {CROSS} and {CROSS}. Notices went to {NUM} properties on {DATE}. So far {NUM} have mowed voluntarily. We have {NUM} properties still over the 8-inch limit and re-inspections are scheduled for {DATE}. I'll refer non-compliant owners to adjudication immediately after. Overall this corridor is trending better than last year.

subject_tmpl: Repeat Violator – Requesting Village Manager Review, {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Karen, I need your guidance on the property at {ADDRESS}. This owner has been cited {NUM} times in the past {DAYS} days across {NUM} separate case files. Each time we get to adjudication they pay the minimum fine and restore minimal compliance, only to reoffend within weeks. The pattern suggests willful non-compliance. I'd like to discuss escalating to a corporate counsel referral or exploring nuisance abatement options. Available to meet at your convenience.
reply_tmpl: Sandra, let's get {DEPT} and corporate counsel on a call before we commit to a strategy. I'll have Tina schedule something for the week of {DATE}.

body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I'm {FIRST} {LAST} and I live at {ADDRESS}. The property next door has had junk cars, broken fencing, and a pile of trash bags in the yard for over {DAYS} days. I've called the hotline twice and nothing seems to happen. This is dragging down our block and I'd appreciate knowing what the Village is actually doing about it. My phone is {PHONE} if someone needs to reach me.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for writing, {FIRST}. I've asked Code Officer Pulaski to pull any open cases for that address and contact you directly by {DATE}. We take property maintenance seriously and I want to make sure you get a real answer.

subject_tmpl: Received a Violation Notice – Need Clarification
body_tmpl: Hello, I received a code violation notice for my property at {ADDRESS} dated {DATE}. The notice says I have {DAYS} days to fix a 'fence in disrepair' but I'm not sure exactly which section of fence is the problem — the notice isn't specific. I've lived here {AGE} years and take good care of my home. Can someone explain what's needed before I spend money on repairs? Case number listed is {CASE}.
reply_tmpl: Good morning, {FIRST}. I've forwarded your message to Officer Pulaski who will call you at {PHONE} by {DATE} to walk through exactly what the inspection found. We want to make sure you have clear guidance. Thank you for reaching out rather than guessing.

body_tmpl: Mayor, there's a boarded-up house at {ADDRESS} that has been vacant for at least {DAYS} days. Kids have been going in through a broken back window and I'm worried about safety. Last week there was a fire in the alley behind it — I think caused by people using it as a hangout spot. Who do I call about this? I'm {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.

Mayor Okonkwo, I'm the owner of {BIZ} at {ADDRESS}. We received a violation notice on {DATE} for 'outdoor storage of merchandise' but the items cited — two propane exchange cages and a sidewalk display rack — are standard retail fixtures used by businesses throughout the Village. The notice references case {CASE}. I'd like to request a meeting to discuss whether this is truly a violation or an inconsistent interpretation of the ordinance.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for contacting us. I've asked Code Officer Pulaski and the Village Manager to review the case. Someone will be in touch by {DATE} to schedule a meeting. We appreciate your patience.

body_tmpl: Sandra, following our walkthrough on {DATE}, I'm making a formal dangerous-structure referral for {ADDRESS}. The roof decking is compromised, there are signs of structural settling in the east foundation wall, and the electrical service entrance is exposed. My inspection report is attached. I recommend we move to a hearing notice promptly — this structure presents a real public safety risk. Corporate counsel should be copied going forward.
reply_tmpl: Rosa, I'll open a dangerous-structure case file today and coordinate with the clerk's office on hearing notice requirements. I'll loop in Karen and the mayor's office per our protocol.

body_tmpl: David, Public Works completed an emergency mow-and-trim abatement at {ADDRESS} on {DATE} after the owner failed to comply with case {CASE}. PW's cost sheet is attached showing {AMOUNT} in labor and equipment time. Please set up a receivable and invoice the owner. Per Village ordinance, if the invoice isn't paid within {DAYS} days, we'll need to proceed with a lien. Let me know if you need anything additional from my case file.

Mayor, attached is our winter vacant-property inspection summary covering {NUM} registered vacant structures in the Village. Of those, {NUM} passed inspection, {NUM} had minor deficiencies requiring correction by {DATE}, and {NUM} have been referred to the building official for further evaluation. Notably, the property at {ADDRESS} has lost its heat source — I've alerted the building official and fire liaison. Overall our vacant property registry program continues to show results compared to prior years.

subject_tmpl: Unpermitted Construction Spotted – {ADDRESS}, Need Code Backup
body_tmpl: Sandra, I issued a stop-work order at {ADDRESS} on {DATE} for unpermitted construction — they're adding a {NUM}-square-foot addition without a permit. The owner was on-site and was uncooperative. I need code enforcement backup in case they continue work. The stop-work order is posted but I have no guarantee they'll comply. Can you schedule a re-inspection for {DATE}? I've notified the police liaison as well.
reply_tmpl: Rosa, I'll be there at {TIME} on {DATE}. If I observe active work in violation of the stop-work order I'll document it for adjudication and loop in Reggie.

body_tmpl: Sandra, we received a FOIA request on {DATE} for all code violation records associated with {ADDRESS} going back {NUM} years. The statutory response deadline is {DATE}. Can you pull and send me everything you have — notices, inspection reports, hearing records, and compliance photos? Please note any records you believe are exempt so I can review before we release. I'll handle the FOIA response letter on my end.
reply_tmpl: Yolanda, I'll compile the file today. I have {NUM} cases on that address. One case has a pending adjudication — I'll flag it for your review. You'll have everything by {DATE}.

body_tmpl: Sandra, the mayor received a call this week from a constituent about the property at {ADDRESS}. He's asking for a status summary so he can respond. Can you send me a brief rundown — when we first cited, what violations, where we are in the process, and expected timeline for resolution? Keep it to a paragraph or two; this is for a constituent callback, not a formal report.
reply_tmpl: Karen, here's the summary: first citation issued {DATE} for {NUM} violations including exterior debris and inoperable vehicle. Owner appeared at adjudication on {DATE}, fined {AMOUNT}. Re-inspection scheduled {DATE}. If not compliant, we proceed to lien referral. I expect resolution by {DATE} barring further delays.

body_tmpl: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, my name is {FIRST} {LAST}. My neighbor at {ADDRESS} installed a 6-foot wood privacy fence along the rear property line last month with no permit that I can find. It also appears to be on my property based on a survey I had done {AGE} years ago. I've tried speaking with them and it hasn't gone well. I'm hoping the Village can inspect and take action. You can reach me at {PHONE}.

subject_tmpl: Emergency Abatement Request – Dumped Waste at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Frank, I need Public Works to schedule an emergency abatement pickup at {ADDRESS}. Code has cited the owner but the pile — furniture, bagged household waste, and construction debris — is attracting rodents and creating a health hazard. I've notified Priya in Health as well. PW needs to handle this on a cost-recovery basis; I'll have the authorization paperwork to you today. Can you get a crew there by {DATE}?
reply_tmpl: Sandra, I can get a crew there {DATE} morning. I'll need the signed authorization before we roll. Send it to me ASAP and I'll have someone confirm the {TIME} arrival.

subject_tmpl: Spring Sweep Launch – Code Enforcement Blitz, {DATE}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I wanted to let you know we're launching our spring code enforcement sweep starting {DATE}. This year we're concentrating on the {STREET} and {CROSS} corridors, focusing on exterior storage, unpermitted structures, and overgrown vegetation. We'll be hitting every block face systematically. PD liaison and health are coordinating so we can triage any multi-department issues in real time. I'll send you an end-of-week summary each Friday through {DATE}.
reply_tmpl: Great initiative, Sandra. Would you be able to give me a two-minute update at the next staff meeting on {DATE}? I want the department heads to hear about the coordinated approach.

body_tmpl: Rosa, I need you to verify whether a permit has been pulled for the garage conversion at {ADDRESS}. The owner told me at inspection on {DATE} that they had 'already filed.' Case {CASE} has a compliance deadline of {DATE}. If a permit application isn't in your system I need to know today so I can keep the adjudication referral on track. Thanks.
reply_tmpl: Sandra, I checked — no application on file for that address. The last permit was {DATE}. The owner did not file. You're clear to proceed with the referral.

body_tmpl: Priya, during my sweep this week I documented {NUM} properties on the {STREET} block between {CROSS} and {CROSS} with unsecured garbage and overfilled containers. Several had open bags attracting wildlife. I've opened code cases on all of them, but given the density and the rodent-attraction risk I wanted to flag it for a health assessment as well. Want to do a joint walkthrough on {DATE}? I'll send you my inspection log.

subject_tmpl: Ordinance Gap – Parking Pads on Parkway
body_tmpl: Karen, I've encountered {NUM} properties this month where owners have laid gravel or pavers on the parkway to create additional parking pads. Our current code doesn't address this scenario clearly — Section 8-{NUM} covers driveways but is silent on parkway surfaces. I believe we need an ordinance amendment to close this gap. I'd also like to add parkway surface restoration as an abatement remedy. Can we put this on the agenda for the next code committee meeting?
reply_tmpl: Sandra, good catch. I'll add it to the {DATE} code committee agenda. Please draft a one-page issue summary and suggested ordinance language so we have something to work from.

body_tmpl: Sandra, the mayor received a constituent email today about the conditions at {ADDRESS} and would like a brief status note before he responds. Specifically: what cases are open, what's the current violation, and what's the expected timeline. Please reply by {TIME} today if at all possible — he has a callback scheduled for {DATE}.
reply_tmpl: Tina, here's the status: {NUM} open cases at that address, most recent inspection {DATE} found exterior debris and an inoperable vehicle. Owner has until {DATE} to comply or it goes to adjudication. I expect resolution within {DAYS} days.

subject_tmpl: Holiday Decoration / Trash Compliance Reminder – Pre-Holiday Note
body_tmpl: Mayor, ahead of the holiday weekend I'm sending courtesy reminder postcards to {NUM} addresses that have had recurring refuse and exterior storage violations. I'd like to get ahead of any post-holiday complaints. I've also notified PW to have an extra recycling drop-off available on {DATE}. No action needed on your end — just a heads-up so you're in the loop if residents mention it.

Officer Pulaski, I'm the facilities manager for {BIZ} at {ADDRESS}. We received a notice for our dumpster enclosure not meeting the screening requirement. We agree it's substandard but the physical layout of our lot makes a compliant enclosure impossible without removing a required fire lane. We'd like to request a variance or discuss an alternative screening solution. Can we schedule an on-site meeting? Best contact is {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for reaching out proactively. Let's schedule a site visit for {DATE} at {TIME}. I'll also loop in the building official and fire liaison so we can assess the fire lane issue at the same time. Please have your site plan available.

subject_tmpl: Drainage Complaint with Possible Code Tie-In – {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Tom, a drainage complaint came in about {ADDRESS} where the property owner appears to have regraded the rear yard in a way that's redirecting runoff onto {ADDRESS}. There's no permit on file for any grading work. I've documented it and opened a code file but I need your engineering eye to determine whether this is also a stormwater issue that requires a corrective drainage plan. Can you take a look before I issue the notice?
reply_tmpl: Sandra, I can do a site review on {DATE}. If the grading altered positive drainage toward the neighboring property that's a stormwater violation on top of the code issue. Let's go together so we can issue a single coordinated notice.

subject_tmpl: Quarter {NUM} Code Enforcement Stats – Highlights for Council
body_tmpl: Mayor, attached is the Q{NUM} code enforcement summary for your council report. Key figures: {NUM} cases opened, {NUM} closed in compliance, {NUM} referred to adjudication, {AMOUNT} in fines assessed. Compliance rate improved {NUM} percentage points over Q{NUM}. The biggest driver was the spring sweep — we closed out {NUM} long-standing cases on the {STREET} corridor. I've also included a chart showing year-over-year case trends if you'd like to highlight that at the meeting on {DATE}.
reply_tmpl: Excellent work, Sandra. I'll highlight the compliance rate improvement at the {DATE} meeting. Please have the chart in presentation format by {DATE}.

subject_tmpl: Fire Run – Possible Code Conditions at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Officer Pulaski, engine responded to {ADDRESS} on {DATE} at {TIME} for a smoke investigation. No fire, but while on scene crew observed extensive hoarding conditions inside the structure — floor-to-ceiling material blocking egress paths, an inoperable smoke detector, and an electrical panel with exposed wiring. We cleared the scene but this property needs a code and building follow-up immediately. I'm attaching our run report. Please coordinate with Rosa Marchetti on the building side.
reply_tmpl: Commander, thank you for the referral. I'll contact Rosa today and we'll do a joint inspection at {ADDRESS} as soon as we can gain access — likely {DATE}. If the owner refuses entry I'll pursue an administrative search warrant. I'll keep you copied.

subject_tmpl: Thank You – Code Issue Resolved on {STREET}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I just wanted to say thank you. I emailed your office {DAYS} days ago about the eyesore property near my home on {STREET} and Officer Pulaski followed up within days. The owner has cleaned up the yard and removed the junk cars. It's remarkable how different the block looks. I know municipal government gets a lot of complaints — please know that your code team earned some real goodwill on our block. {FIRST} {LAST}
reply_tmpl: Dear {FIRST}, thank you for taking the time to write. I'm so glad to hear the situation was resolved. I'll be sure to share your kind words with Officer Pulaski and the whole code enforcement team — they work hard and it means a great deal to hear it made a difference.
